question: Return a table with the top 20 product_id values by delivered net revenue, including columns product_id, delivered_units, delivered_net_revenue, and avg_unit_price.
sql_query:
SELECT oi.product_id,
       SUM(oi.quantity) AS delivered_units,
       SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity - COALESCE(oi.discount_amount, 0)) AS delivered_net_revenue,
       CASE WHEN SUM(oi.quantity) > 0
            THEN SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity - COALESCE(oi.discount_amount, 0)) / SUM(oi.quantity)
            ELSE 0
       END AS avg_unit_price
FROM core.order_items AS oi
JOIN core.orders AS o ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'
GROUP BY oi.product_id
ORDER BY delivered_net_revenue DESC
LIMIT 20
pandas_sql_query: SELECT oi.product_id, SUM(CASE WHEN o.order_status = 'delivered' THEN oi.quantity ELSE 0 END) AS delivered_units, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN o.order_status = 'delivered' THEN oi.quantity * oi.unit_price - oi.discount_amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS delivered_net_revenue, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN o.order_status = 'delivered' THEN oi.unit_price END), 2) AS avg_unit_price FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o USING (order_id) GROUP BY 1 HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN o.order_status = 'delivered' THEN oi.quantity ELSE 0 END) > 0 ORDER BY delivered_net_revenue DESC, product_id LIMIT 20
